shower.thought: You spend the first part of your life wishing you were older and the second part wishing you were younger, but you're never actually satisfied with the age you are.
The people you loved 10 years ago are still alive somewhere living entire lives without you, and you're doing the same to them.
We call it "falling asleep" but it's actually the most active thing your brain does all night.
Your phone knows you better than you know yourself, which means a stranger reading your data would understand your patterns before you would.
The only time you see your face is in reflections and photos—never as others see it, which means you're living with a slightly incorrect image of yourself.
You can feel pain in a tooth you lost years ago because the nerve endings still exist in your brain.
If you've ever felt like someone "gets you," they probably just tolerate your quirks better than most people, and you called it understanding.
You're constantly worried about things that would solve themselves if you just waited long enough, yet somehow waiting feels impossible.
Dead languages are just conversations nobody was interested in continuing.
The scariest part of getting older isn't aging itself—it's realizing that the things you're worried about now probably won't matter, and you won't know which ones until it's too late.
You assume people remember things you said to them, but most people are too busy remembering the things they said that embarrassed them.
Ambition is just controlled anxiety channeled into a direction you chose.
You're essentially a house haunted by a consciousness that can't explain why it's there.
The reason you can't tickle yourself is the same reason you can't surprise yourself—your brain needs an opponent.
Everyone's life is made up mostly of boring moments, but we've only ever seen the highlight reels of everyone else's, so we compare our full footage to their edited version.
If you've ever felt like an imposter, you might just be more honest with yourself than people who feel confident.
We mourn celebrities we never knew because we're mourning the loss of the person we imagined them to be.
Your sense of smell is connected directly to memory because your brain decided that what you smell matters more than what you see—maybe that's a hint about what actually matters.
You can't know if someone else's red is the same as your red, which means you might never truly see the world the way anyone else does.
The worst pain isn't usually the sharp kind—it's the kind you get used to.

motivational.thought: Your limitation is not your circumstances. It's your willingness to be limited by them. — Unknown
The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones. — Confucius
You cannot control the wind, but you can adjust your sails. — Dolly Parton
Success is not final, failure is not fatal. It is the courage to continue that counts. — Winston Churchill
The only way to do great work is to love what you do. — Steve Jobs
Between stimulus and response there is a space. In that space is our power to choose our response. — Viktor Frankl
Your potential is endless. Your excuses are not. — Unknown
The years teach much which the days never know. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
We are not makers of history. We are made by history. — Martin Luther King Jr.
Everything you want is on the other side of fear. — Jack Canfield
Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most. — Unknown
The greatest glory in living lies not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall. — Nelson Mandela
You miss 100% of the shots you don't take. — Wayne Gretzky
Patience is not passive; it is concentrated strength. — Edward G. Bulwer-Lytton
The obstacle in the path becomes the path. — Marcus Aurelius
Comfort is the enemy of greatness, but growth is the reward of discomfort. — Unknown
A man is but the product of his thoughts; what he thinks, he becomes. — Mahatma Gandhi
The only real failure is the failure to try. — George Edward Woodberry
Your limitation is only your current perspective. Expand it. — Unknown
Excellence is a habit, not an act. — Aristotle

darkjoke.thought:
I told my friend he was being paranoid about his wife cheating.
He said, "You're right, I should probably stop worrying and just accept it."

I'm reading a book on anti-gravity.
I can't put it down, but not for the reasons the author intended.

My therapist asked why I sabotage every good relationship.
I said, "That's a great question. Same time next week?"

People always say they'd take a bullet for their kids.
I'm just saying, statistically, that seems unlikely.
I have a stepladder because my real ladder left when I was just a kid.

My psychiatrist prescribed me exercise for my anxiety.
Now I'm just an anxious person who's tired.

I tried to make a joke about my own death once.
My therapist said I was being too dark.
I said, "You're right, I should lighten up before I hang myself."
The hardest part about being an atheist at a funeral is not laughing when they say he's in a better place.

I don't fear death itself.
It's the funeral slideshow that terrifies me.
My life is like a horror movie, except nobody's watching and I can't turn it off.

I told my therapist I keep having violent thoughts.
She asked if I've considered journaling instead.
I said, "I have. But my hands keep shaking."

People say money can't buy happiness, but it can buy a jetski.
Have you ever seen someone frowning on a jetski?
You're right, that's because they're drowning.

My doctor told me I have six months to live.
I said I couldn't afford the treatment.
He said, "In that case, you have three months."

I'm not saying I have commitment issues.
I'm saying I have commitment experience that ended badly enough to learn from.

My friends worry I don't take anything seriously.
They stopped worrying after the incident.
I still don't know what incident they meant.

Dating in your thirties is like online shopping.
You know exactly what you want, everything's either taken or defective, and you'll probably settle for something you saw four years ago.

I used to be addicted to soap.
It's a long story.

My therapist says I need to work on my self-esteem.
I told her, "You're right, I do suck."
Apparently that's not the improvement she was hoping for.

When I was a kid, my parents said I could be anything I wanted.
Turns out they just meant employed.

I asked my doctor if I was going to recover.
He said, "Only time will tell."
I said, "Can you be more specific?"
He said, "Sure. You're not."
